_about: Maps OSINT finding 'signals' to MITRE ATT&CK techniques. Each signal key is produced by eidolon/agent/nodes.py:_extract_attack_signals() from scan results. Add a signal block, then add the matching extraction rule. This file doubles as ATT&CK study notes — every entry links to the official technique page.

password_in_breach.name: Gather Victim Identity Information: Credentials
password_in_breach.what_it_is: Collecting a target's leaked usernames and passwords from breach dumps to prepare account-takeover attacks.
password_in_breach.why_this_finding: Your password (or its hash) was exposed in one or more breaches, handing an attacker a credential to try directly or crack offline.

password_in_breach.name: Brute Force: Credential Stuffing
password_in_breach.what_it_is: Replaying a leaked email+password pair against many other sites, betting the password was reused.
password_in_breach.why_this_finding: Your credentials are in public breach dumps, so an attacker can stuff them into other services you use — anywhere you reused that password is exposed.

infostealer_log.name: Credentials from Password Stores
infostealer_log.what_it_is: Stealing passwords saved in web browsers, password managers, and the OS keychain.
infostealer_log.why_this_finding: Infostealer malware that ran on the victim's machine dumped every password the browser had saved at once — not one site's password, the whole store.

infostealer_log.name: Steal Web Session Cookie
infostealer_log.what_it_is: Stealing a browser's active session cookies to hijack a logged-in session without the password or a 2FA prompt.
infostealer_log.why_this_finding: The same malware exfiltrated live session cookies, so an attacker can resume an authenticated session even with 2FA enabled.

infostealer_log.name: Valid Accounts
infostealer_log.what_it_is: Logging in with legitimate stolen credentials instead of breaking in — the activity looks normal and is hard to detect.
infostealer_log.why_this_finding: With the stolen passwords and cookies, an attacker signs in to the victim's real accounts directly; there is nothing to 'exploit' and little to flag.
